transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hi, this is James calling from Wati. Am I speaking with Deepak?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yes, this is Deepak.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hi Deepak, I'm following up on the ticket you raised yesterday about your chatbot not responding to customer messages. Can you give me your account number so I can look into this?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Sure, it's ACC-77421.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you. Let me pull that up. So the chatbot isn't responding at all, or is it giving wrong responses?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: It's not responding at all. Customers send messages and the bot just doesn't reply. We've been handling everything manually since yesterday which is killing our team.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I understand. Let me check your chatbot configuration. Okay, I can see your chatbot flow here. It looks like the trigger keyword was changed — it was set to 'hi' and 'hello' before, but now it's set to 'start'. So the bot only activates when someone types 'start'.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Oh. That might have been one of our new team members. They were editing the flow yesterday.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That would explain it. I'd recommend reverting the trigger keywords back to your original set. Would you like me to walk you through that?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Actually, can you just fix it on your end? I'm not near a computer right now.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can update the trigger keywords for you. I'll set them back to 'hi', 'hello', 'hey', and 'start' so it covers all the common greetings. Does that work?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yes, that sounds perfect.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Done. I've updated the keywords. The chatbot should start responding again within a couple of minutes as the changes propagate. Can you have someone on your team test it in about five minutes?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Sure, I'll ask my colleague to test it. Is there a way to prevent this from happening again? Like restricting who can edit the chatbot?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yes, you can set role-based permissions in your team settings. You can restrict chatbot editing to admin users only. I'd recommend doing that if you have newer team members who might accidentally change things.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Good to know. I'll set that up.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Sounds good. Do you have any other questions?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: No, that's all. Thanks James.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're welcome. Have a good day, Deepak.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: You too, bye.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Bye!
